 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ank","title":"[Diary] loadDiaryEntry - Read from disk","description":"Read and validate diary entry from disk.

Function Signature:
async function loadDiaryEntry(diaryPath: string): Promise<DiaryEntry>

Process:
1. Verify file exists at diaryPath
2. Read file contents as UTF-8 string
3. Parse JSON string to object
4. Validate against DiaryEntrySchema using Zod
5. Return validated DiaryEntry

Validation Features:
- Type checking for all fields
- Required field validation
- Date format validation for timestamp
- Array content validation
- Status enum validation (success/failure/mixed)

Error Handling:
- FileNotFoundError if path doesn't exist
- JSONParseError if invalid JSON
- SchemaValidationError if missing/invalid fields
- Detailed error messages for debugging

Schema Migration:
- If old diary version detected, apply migration
- Add default values for new optional fields
- Preserve backward compatibility

Key Features:
- Full schema validation ensures type safety
- Detailed validation errors for debugging
- Migration support for schema evolution
- UTF-8 encoding handling

Use Cases:
- Loading diary for evidence correlation
- Reading diaries for stats computation
- Fetching diary for display in UI
- Validating diary integrity

Output:
Returns fully validated DiaryEntry object ready for use.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:09.388329-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:16.248993-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:10.583918-05:00"}

## Functions to Test (test/utils.path.test.ts)

### resolveRepoDir(startDir?: string)

### resolveGlobalDir()

### ensureDir(dir: string)

- [ ] No-op if already exists
- [ ] Create nested directories
- [ ] Handle permission errors gracefully

### fileExists(path: string)

- [ ] Return false for non-existent

### atomicWrite(filePath: string, content: string)

- [ ] Set correct permissions (0o600)
- [ ] Clean up temp on failure
- [ ] Handle directory not exists

## Test Approach
- Use real file system with temp directories
- beforeAll: create temp dir
- afterAll: cleanup temp dir
- Each test isolated to subdirectory

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-apt","title":"[Cass] cassSearch - Execute cass search with options","description":"Implement cassSearch() to execute cass search with comprehensive options.

## Signature
```typescript
interface CassSearchOptions {
  limit?: number;
  days?: number;
  agent?: string;
  workspace?: string;
  fields?: string;
  maxTokens?: number;
  highlight?: boolean;
  timeout?: number;
}

interface CassHit {
  sessionPath: string;
  lineNumber: number;
  content: string;
  timestamp?: string;
  score?: number;
}

async function cassSearch(
  query: string, 
  options: CassSearchOptions = {},
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassHit[]>
```

## CLI Command
`cass search "{query}" --robot [--limit N] [--days N] [--agent A] [--workspace W] [--fields F] [--max-tokens N] [--highlight] [--timeout N]`

## Implementation
- Build command with all provided options
- Use execFile() with maxBuffer: 50MB for large results
- Parse JSON output from --robot flag
- Return array of CassHit objects

## Error Handling
Check exit code and handle intelligently:
- Exit 0: Success, parse and return hits
- Exit 3: Index missing - rebuild index, then retry once
- Exit 10: Timeout - reduce limit by 50%, retry once
- Exit 4: Not found - return empty array
- Other errors: Log and return empty array

## Options Details
- limit: Max hits to return (default: 10)
- days: Only search last N days
- agent: Filter by agent name (e.g., "claude-code")
- workspace: Filter by workspace path
- fields: Comma-separated fields to search
- maxTokens: Truncate results to N tokens
- highlight: Include highlighting markers in results
- timeout: Command timeout in seconds

## Usage
```typescript
const hits = await cassSearch("bug fix", { 
  limit: 20, 
  days: 7, 
  agent: "claude-code" 
});
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.153732-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:10.078585-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:00.15937-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ar2","title":"[Curator] invertToAntiPattern - Convert harmful rule to pitfall","description":"Convert a harmful bullet into an anti-pattern (learning from mistakes).

Signature:
function invertToAntiPattern(bullet: PlaybookBullet): PlaybookBullet

KEY INNOVATION: Instead of deleting harmful rules, INVERT them to preserve learning.

Process:
1. Create new bullet with inverted semantics:
   - content: Prefix with 'AVOID: ' or 'DON\'T: '
   - Example: 'Use any type' → 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason'
   
2. Set bullet fields:
   - type: 'anti-pattern'
   - kind: 'anti_pattern'
   - category: same as original
   - maturity: 'candidate' (starts fresh)
   - state: 'active'
   
3. Preserve provenance:
   - source: copy from original
   - originalId: link back to harmful bullet
   - createdAt: current timestamp
   - derivedFrom: original bullet ID
   
4. Reset feedback (fresh start):
   - helpfulCount: 0
   - harmfulCount: 0
   - helpfulEvents: []
   - harmfulEvents: []
   
5. Return new anti-pattern bullet

Why inversion matters:
- Preserves WHAT NOT TO DO
- Prevents future developers from making same mistake
- Converts negative experience into positive guidance
- Maintains complete learning history

Example transformation:
Original (harmful): 'Always use any type for flexibility'
Anti-pattern: 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason. Prefer specific types for type safety.'

The anti-pattern must earn trust like any other candidate bullet.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.707658-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:14:16.240924-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:14:09.899034-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-f87n","title":"[Test] Helpers: Test logger with structured output","description":"Create test/helpers/logger.ts with:

## TestLogger Class

\`\`\`typescript
interface LogEntry {
  timestamp: string;
  level: "DEBUG" | "INFO" | "WARN" | "ERROR";
  message: string;
  data?: unknown;
  testName?: string;
}

class TestLogger {
  private logs: LogEntry[] = [];
  private testName: string;
  
  constructor(testName: string);
  
  debug(message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  info(message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  warn(message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  error(message: string, data?: unknown): void;
  
  // Get all logs for this test
  getLogs(): LogEntry[];
  
  // Format logs as readable string
  format(): string;
  
  // Clear logs (call in afterEach)
  clear(): void;
  
  // Dump to console (call on test failure)
  dump(): void;
}
\`\`\`

## Global Logger

\`\`\`typescript
// Singleton for current test
let currentLogger: TestLogger | null = null;

export function setTestLogger(logger: TestLogger): void;
export function getTestLogger(): TestLogger;
export function log(level: LogLevel, message: string, data?: unknown): void;
\`\`\`

## Integration with Bun Test

\`\`\`typescript
// In test setup
beforeEach(() => {
  const testName = expect.getState().currentTestName;
  setTestLogger(new TestLogger(testName));
});

afterEach(() => {
  if (expect.getState().assertionCalls !== expect.getState().numPassingAsserts) {
    getTestLogger().dump(); // Dump on failure
  }
  getTestLogger().clear();
});
\`\`\`

## Output Format
\`\`\`
[2025-12-07T20:15:30.123Z] [INFO] [test: creates playbook] Starting test
[2025-12-07T20:15:30.125Z] [DEBUG] [test: creates playbook] Created temp dir: /tmp/cass-test-xxx
[2025-12-07T20:15:30.130Z] [INFO] [test: creates playbook] Saved playbook with 5 bullets
\`\`\`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:22:43.325359-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:13:03.907274-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:12:33.8489-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-faw9","title":"[Integration] Concurrent Access - File Locking Tests","description":"## Target: Integration tests for concurrent access handling

### Test Scenarios:

1. **Concurrent Playbook Writes**
   - Start 5 parallel mark commands
   - All target same playbook
   - File locking prevents corruption
   - All events recorded

2. **Read During Write**
   - Start long write operation
   - Attempt read during write
   - Read should wait or get consistent state
   - No partial reads

3. **Multiple Process Access**
   - Spawn multiple cm processes
   - All modify same files
   - Verify final state consistent
   - No lost updates

4. **Lock Timeout Handling**
   - Simulate stuck lock
   - New process should timeout
   - Error message helpful
   - Recovery possible

5. **Atomic Write Verification**
   - Interrupt write mid-operation
   - File should be unchanged OR complete
   - No partial writes
   - Temp files cleaned up

### Test Implementation:
```typescript
describe('concurrent access', () => {
  it('handles parallel writes', async () => {
    const promises = Array(5).fill(null).map((_, i) => 
      runCommand(['mark', 'bullet-id', 'helpful', '--context', `writer-${i}`])
    );
    await Promise.all(promises);
    
    const playbook = await loadPlaybook(testPath);
    expect(playbook.bullets[0].feedbackEvents).toHaveLength(5);
  });
});
```

### Logging:
- Lock acquisition times
- Lock wait times
- Process IDs
- Operation sequences","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:30:15.205956-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T14:27:02.248868-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T14:27:02.248868-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-faw9","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-uufj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:35.701868-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-faw9","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-mmxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:35.899922-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-faw9","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-zc1d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:33:04.194207-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-fem1","title":"[Unit] validate.ts - Input Validation & Sanitization","description":"## Target: src/validate.ts - Validation Module

### Validation Result Schema:

- Boundary cases (min/max lengths)
- Unicode and special characters","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:28:36.92667-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T14:22:22.774731-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T14:22:22.774731-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-fem1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-uufj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:17.532661-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-fem1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-mmxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:59.887193-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-fh0","title":"[LLM] llmWithFallback - Multi-provider fallback","description":"Function signature:
async function llmWithFallback<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  prompt: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<T>

Implementation:
1. Try primary provider (from config.provider)
2. If fails:
   a. Check which other providers have API keys set
   b. Try them in order: anthropic → openai → google
   c. Use same prompt and schema
   d. Return first successful result
3. If all providers fail, throw combined error

Provider ordering rationale:
- Anthropic: Strong reasoning, structured output
- OpenAI: Fast, reliable
- Google: Fallback option

Resilience: Protects against provider-specific outages.

Use case: Production systems where uptime matters more than specific provider.

Note: Config should have a 'fallbackEnabled' flag - only use this if user opts in.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseSnow","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:10.574337-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:14:06.43923-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:14:05.645003-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-uj8","title":"[Validator] evidenceCountGate - Pre-LLM evidence filter","description":"Implement the evidenceCountGate function - Pre-LLM evidence filter that uses simple counts from cass hits + diary status correlation.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Pre-LLM filter: check if there's enough evidence to even consider this rule.
 * Uses simple counts from cass hits + diary status correlation.
 */
async function evidenceCountGate(
  content: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<EvidenceGateResult> {
  const keywords = extractKeywords(content);
  const hits = await cassSearch(keywords.join(" "), { limit: 20, days: 90 });

  if (hits.length === 0) {
    return {
      passed: true,  // No evidence either way - allow as draft
      reason: "No historical evidence found, proposing as draft",
      suggestedState: "draft",
      sessionCount: 0, successCount: 0, failureCount: 0
    };
  }

  // Group by unique session
  const sessions = new Set(hits.map(h => h.source_path));

  // Correlate with diaries to get success/failure counts
  let successCount = 0;
  let failureCount = 0;

  for (const sessionPath of sessions) {
    const diary = await findDiaryBySession(sessionPath);
    if (diary) {
      if (diary.status === "success") successCount++;
      else if (diary.status === "failure") failureCount++;
    }
  }

  const sessionCount = sessions.size;

  // Gate 1: Single session - too early to generalize
  if (sessionCount === 1) {
    return {
      passed: true,
      reason: "Single session only - proposing as draft for further validation",
      suggestedState: "draft",
      sessionCount, successCount, failureCount
    };
  }

  // Gate 2: More failures than successes - REJECT
  if (failureCount > successCount && failureCount >= 2) {
    return {
      passed: false,
      reason: `Pattern appears in ${failureCount} failed sessions vs ${successCount} successful - rejecting`,
      suggestedState: "draft",
      sessionCount, successCount, failureCount
    };
  }

  // Gate 3: Strong evidence - allow as active candidate
  if (successCount >= 3 && failureCount === 0) {
    return {
      passed: true,
      reason: `Strong evidence: ${successCount} successful sessions, 0 failures`,
      suggestedState: "active",
      sessionCount, successCount, failureCount
    };
  }

  // Default: pass to LLM, suggest draft
  return {
    passed: true,
    reason: `Mixed evidence: ${sessionCount} sessions (${successCount} success, ${failureCount} failure)`,
    suggestedState: "draft",
    sessionCount, successCount, failureCount
  };
}
```

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Saves LLM cost by filtering out obviously bad rules
- Improves quality by rejecting patterns from failed sessions
- Fast heuristic-based validation before expensive LLM call
- Scientific validation: rules must be supported by historical evidence

VALIDATION GATES:
1. Single session → draft (too early to generalize)
2. More failures than successes → REJECT
3. 3+ successes, 0 failures → active candidate
4. Mixed evidence → pass to LLM as draft

DEPENDENCIES:
- extractKeywords() - Extract search terms from rule
- cassSearch() - Search historical sessions
- findDiaryBySession() - Get success/failure status

LOCATION:
- src/validator/evidence-gate.ts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:51.519581-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:53:50.976175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:53:11.224354-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-uj99","title":"[EPIC] Complete Unit Test Coverage Without Mocks","description":"# Epic: Complete Unit Test Coverage

## Goal
Achieve comprehensive unit test coverage for ALL source modules without relying on mocks or fake implementations. Tests should exercise real code paths with isolated test environments (temp directories, test configs).

## Current State (from analysis)
- **67% command coverage** (16/24 commands tested)
- **81% core module coverage** (21/26 modules tested)
- **Critical gaps**: cm.ts (CLI router), orchestrator.ts (reflection loop), 8 commands with zero tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 100% of src/commands/*.ts have dedicated test files
- [ ] 100% of src/*.ts core modules have dedicated test files
- [ ] No test file uses module-level mocking (vi.mock, jest.mock)
- [ ] All tests use withTempCassHome or similar isolation
- [ ] Test-to-source ratio >= 1.5:1 for complex modules

## Subtasks (in dependency order)
1. P0: Test cm.ts CLI router
2. P0: Test orchestrator.ts reflection loop
3. P0: Test why.ts command
4. P1: Test privacy.ts command

## Anti-patterns to avoid

- NO fake LLM responses (use real prompts with deterministic test data)
- NO hardcoded paths (use temp directories)
- NO process.exit() in tests (use  {"id":"cass_memory_system-ulu7","title":"Epic: Agent UX Improvements for Discoverability and Consistency","description":"## Overview

This epic addresses several agent-friendliness issues identified during comprehensive code review. The goal is to make cass-memory more intuitive and discoverable for AI coding agents, who are the primary users.

## Problem Statement

AI agents learn patterns from existing commands. When command structures are inconsistent, agents experience friction and make mistakes. Currently:

1. **Inconsistent command patterns**: `playbook` and `privacy` use subcommands (`playbook list`, `privacy status`) but `onboard` uses flags (`--status`, `--sample`). This inconsistency creates cognitive load.

2. **Solo user gap**: The quickstart documentation tells agents "don't run reflect manually" but doesn't explain what solo users (who are both agent AND operator) should do instead.

3. **Undocumented options**: The `mark --harmful --reason` flag accepts specific enum values (caused_bug, wasted_time, etc.) but these aren't documented in --help.

4. **Command overload**: The `onboard` command has ~12 mutually exclusive flags representing different workflow steps, making it hard to discover and use.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All commands follow consistent subcommand patterns where appropriate
- [ ] Quickstart includes clear guidance for solo users
- [ ] All enum/option values are documented in --help
- [ ] Agent can discover `onboard` functionality via `cm onboard --help` seeing a list of subcommands
- [ ] Backwards compatibility maintained with deprecation warnings

## Design Principles

1. **Consistency over convenience**: Match established patterns even if flags seem "simpler"
2. **Discoverability**: Agents should be able to explore via --help
3. **Backwards compatibility**: Old syntax works with deprecation warnings
4. **Progressive disclosure**: Simple use cases are simple, complex use cases are possible

## Implementation Phases

**Phase 1 (Quick Wins)**: Documentation fixes - no code changes to command behavior
**Phase 2 (Onboard Refactor)**: Convert onboard flags to subcommands with deprecation layer
**Phase 3 (Polish)**: Optional improvements to outcome command UX

## Dependencies

None - this epic is independent of other work.

## Risks

- Breaking change for users with scripts using old onboard flags
  - Mitigation: 6-month deprecation period with clear warnings
- Increased code complexity from deprecation layer
  - Mitigation: Remove deprecated code in next major version","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T12:16:21.55085-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T12:51:20.987684-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T12:51:20.987684-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ulu7","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-e6o7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T12:19:00.831182-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ulu7","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-b9dr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T12:19:00.8827-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ulu7","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-uom9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T12:19:00.929117-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ulu7","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-84gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T12:19:00.978662-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-umj0","title":"[Test] Helpers: Playbook and bullet factories","description":"Create test/helpers/factories.ts with:

## Functions

### createTestBullet(overrides?: Partial<PlaybookBullet>): PlaybookBullet
- Returns valid bullet with sensible defaults
- Generates unique ID if not provided
- Sets timestamps to now()
- Merges overrides

### createTestPlaybook(options?: { bulletCount?: number; withDeprecated?: boolean }): Playbook
- Returns valid playbook with configurable bullets
- Uses createTestBullet internally
- Generates metadata timestamps

### createTestDiary(overrides?: Partial<DiaryEntry>): DiaryEntry
- Returns valid diary with realistic content
- Sets session path to temp path
- Generates unique ID

### createTestConfig(overrides?: Partial<Config>): Config
- Returns valid config with defaults
- No LLM API keys (tests shouldn't call LLM)
- Uses temp paths for playbook/diary

### createFeedbackEvent(type: "helpful" | "harmful", daysAgo?: number): FeedbackEvent
- Creates feedback event with timestamp
- daysAgo parameter for testing decay

## Design Principles
- All factories return VALID objects (pass schema validation)
- No external dependencies
- Deterministic (same input = same output, except timestamps)
- Composable (use factories in other factories)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:22:43.345267-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:13:03.928607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:12:33.844965-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-un2k","title":"[UX][onboarding] Restructure cm --help for progressive disclosure","description":"Problem
- The CLI has many commands, but the product story is “one command for agents” (`cm context`).
- Default help output doesn’t strongly guide new users and can feel overwhelming.

What to do
- Improve help information architecture:
  - Put agent workflow first (context + quickstart)
  - Group operator/maintenance commands separately (init/doctor/reflect/playbook)
  - Group advanced/rare commands last (serve/outcome/audit/validate)
  - Add 3-5 high-quality examples that match README

Constraints
- Keep commander usage intact; use built-in help hooks (`addHelpText`, `showHelpAfterError`, etc.).
- Use invoked binary name (depends on naming task).","design":"Implementation sketch
- Use commander’s `program.addHelpText("after", ...)` to print curated sections.
- Consider `program.showHelpAfterError(true)` and `program.showSuggestionAfterError(true)`.","acceptance_criteria":"- `cm --help` is noticeably clearer and highlights the “one command” path.
- Help examples are copy/paste correct.
- Errors show help suggestions without dumping stack traces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:20:47.859404-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T17:43:09.318407-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T17:43:09.318407-05:00","labels":["cli","onboarding","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-un2k","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-1ojv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:21:00.001777-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-un2k","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-2yzr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:22:17.153805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
